title: Next Pointer Binary Tree

starterCode.python:
# Definition for a binary tree node

#         self.val = x
#         self.left = None
#         self.right = None
#         self.next = None

class Solution:
    # @param A, a tree node
    # @return nothing
    def connect(self, A):

hints: Use level-order traversal to connect nodes at the same level.
You can use a dummy node to help connect nodes at each level.
Traverse level by level, connecting left and right children of each node.
For each level, use a temporary pointer to build the chain of next pointers.
After processing a level, move to the next level using the dummy node's next pointer.
The dummy node helps you keep track of the start of the next level.
Since it's a perfect binary tree, every node has either 0 or 2 children.
